Graded Novelty Enoding Task - 4 shapes version - judge difference

Exclusions

We excluded 11 participants due to a high number of missed responses in the study phase (<= 10).

We excluded 6 participants due to low recognition rates (<= 20).

We excluded 2 participants due to inattention to task details as shown by <5 mean difference judged for prototype - prototype comparisons.

This left 42-42 participants in the analysis.

The identifiers are: 566_462, 565_459, 564_458, 562_456, 561_453, 560_451, 559_449, 557_435, 554_423, 551_407, 547_385, 544_371, 543_367, 540_343, 539_342, 536_324, 535_323, 534_308, 533_262, 532_259, 531_253, 530_240, 529_215, 528_214, 527_213, 526_212, 524_210, 523_208, 522_207, 520_205, 518_204, 519_203, 517_202, 516_200, 515_199, 511_193, 508_192, 510_191, 504_190, 505_189, 507_187, 503_180

Study phase

Participants make a judgement of the difference between a prototype and a novel picture.

Test phase

Test phase was the same as before: participants see the novel pictures from the study phase with distractors from the same categories. They signal if they think the picture is an old one from the study phase, or a novel distractor.

## 
##  Paired t-test
## 
## data:  a and b
## t = 0.83958, df = 209, p-value = 0.4021
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.1540642  0.3826356
## sample estimates:
## mean of the differences 
##               0.1142857
## 
## Cohen's d
## 
## d estimate: 0.05455349 (negligible)
## 95 percent confidence interval:

## -0.07326452  0.18237150
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  d
## W = 0.97268, p-value = 0.000421
